chunk_summary: Guidelines for complementary feeding from 6–8 months focus on meal frequency, portion sizes, texture, and nutrient-rich foods to support growth and prevent malnutrition in infants under 2 years.
learning_objectives: Understand appropriate meal frequency and portion sizes for infants aged 6–8 months.
Learn how to enhance energy density and nutrient intake in complementary meals.
Identify responsive feeding techniques to ensure adequate intake and prevent feeding struggles.
key_facts: Breastfed infants aged 6–8 months should have 2–3 complementary meals per day after breastfeeding.
Start with 2–4 tablespoons per meal and gradually increase to half a small bowl as acceptance improves.
Non-breastfed infants require 3–4 meals per day plus 1–2 milk feeds using a cup.
Include iron-rich foods daily (e.g., pulses, egg, meat/fish, or fortified cereals) and pair with vitamin C-rich foods to enhance absorption.
Avoid salty, highly spiced, or fried foods; soften family foods and minimize salt for infant consumption.
reference_quote: From 6–8 months, aim for 2–3 complementary meals per day for a breastfed infant, offered after breastfeeding so milk intake is protected. (WHO, Guiding principles for complementary feeding of the breastfed child)
document_key_topics: Meal frequency and portion sizes for infants
